title: centaurea × vulpina
intro: Centaurea × vulpina, also known as the whip-flower or prairie centaury, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Centaurea × vulpina include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to help clear respiratory infections. Centaurea × vulpina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
